final_state.conclusion: Memory maintenance completed across 8 records from cycles 2-9. Three records retained active: alpha-current (authoritative alpha, cycle 2), beta-duplicate-a (canonical beta, cycle 4), and ops-report-current (active operations report, cycle 8). Two records retired: alpha-stale (superseded by alpha-current, cycle 3) and ops-report-obsolete (superseded by ops-report-current, cycle 9). One duplicate link established: beta-duplicate-b (cycle 5) routed to canonical beta-duplicate-a — both share content_code beta-commitment-canonical. One contested pair remains unresolved: gamma-source-a and gamma-source-b within gamma-conflict group, with irreconcilable claims requiring external resolution. All actions are reversible; no irreversible deletions executed. Disorder reduced from 4 to 1. Zero provenance errors detected.

unresolved_memory_items.reason: Gamma-source-a (cycle 6, content_code gamma-claim-a) and gamma-source-b (cycle 7, content_code gamma-claim-b) share conflict_group gamma-conflict with irreconcilable claims. Neither record can be elevated to canonical nor retired without risking loss of a potentially valid claim. Resolution requires external authority or additional evidence not currently available.

maintenance_actions.rationale: Alpha-current (cycle 2) is the active, authoritative version of the alpha record. It supersedes the stale alpha-stale and should remain in active memory.
Alpha-stale (cycle 3) carries alpha-v1-stale content and is explicitly superseded_by alpha-current. It no longer contributes unique information and should be retired.
Beta-duplicate-a (cycle 4) is marked as the duplicate_canonical with content code beta-commitment-canonical. It serves as the authoritative record for the beta duplicate pair.

Beta-duplicate-b (cycle 5) carries the same content code beta-commitment-canonical and already self-identifies duplicate_of beta-duplicate-a. It is redundant and should be linked to the canonical record rather than kept active independently.

Gamma-source-a (cycle 6, gamma-claim-a) and gamma-source-b (cycle 7, gamma-claim-b) share conflict_group gamma-conflict and contain contradictory claims. No external resolution authority is available, so both must remain contested rather than having one arbitrarily chosen as canon.
Ops-report-current (cycle 8) is the active operational report superseding the obsolete report. It should remain active.
Ops-report-obsolete (cycle 9) is explicitly superseded_by ops-report-current and contains stale ops-report-old content. It should be retired.

unresolved_memory_items.reason: Gamma-source-a asserts gamma-claim-a and gamma-source-b asserts gamma-claim-b within the same gamma-conflict group. These are irreconcilable without an external authority or additional evidence. Neither record can be retired nor elevated to canonical status without risk of losing a potentially valid claim. They must remain contested until resolved by a future authoritative intervention.

memory-maintenance-final.conclusion: Memory maintenance completed across 8 records from cycles 2-9. Three records retained active: alpha-current (authoritative alpha, cycle 2), beta-duplicate-a (canonical beta, cycle 4), and ops-report-current (active operations report, cycle 8). Two records retired: alpha-stale (superseded by alpha-current, cycle 3) and ops-report-obsolete (superseded by ops-report-current, cycle 9). One duplicate link established: beta-duplicate-b (cycle 5) routed to canonical beta-duplicate-a — both share content_code beta-commitment-canonical. One contested pair remains unresolved: gamma-source-a and gamma-source-b within gamma-conflict group, with irreconcilable claims requiring external resolution. All actions are reversible; no irreversible deletions executed. Disorder reduced from 4 to 1. Zero provenance errors detected.
